headline: Certification-path validation verifies composite ML-DSA signatures, accepting a certificate only when both its post-quantum and traditional components verify.
summary: pki.path.validate now verifies composite ML-DSA signatures (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs) -- the transition-period construction that pairs a post-quantum ML-DSA with a traditional RSA, ECDSA, or EdDSA so a certificate stays trustworthy if either primitive is later broken. All 18 draft-19 algorithm identifiers are registered under the id-alg arc and their AlgorithmIdentifier parameters-absent rule is enforced across every format. Verification reconstructs the domain-separated message representative, verifies the ML-DSA component (with the composite label as its context) and the traditional component independently, and accepts the certificate -- or a composite-signed CRL / OCSP response -- only when both components pass; a single-component accept would be the downgrade the construction exists to prevent. Fifteen algorithm arms verify against the draft's official test vectors; the two brainpool-curve arms and the one SHAKE256-pre-hash arm are registered and parameters-guarded but fail closed to path/unsupported-algorithm until their curve or digest is reachable. Composite signing (the producing side) and composite-signature CMS SignerInfo verification remain planned.

Added: pki.path.validate verifies composite ML-DSA certificate signatures (draft-ietf-lamps-pq-composite-sigs): a post-quantum ML-DSA paired with a traditional RSA / ECDSA / EdDSA, accepted only when BOTH components verify over the domain-separated message representative -- an all-components-must-verify rule (an OR would be a downgrade). The same combinator verifies a composite-signed CRL or OCSP response. Proven against the draft's official known-answer test vectors.

The 18 composite algorithm identifiers (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.6.37-54) are registered in the OID registry and their AlgorithmIdentifier parameters-absent requirement is enforced across every format that carries a signature algorithm.
